parse_warnings.message: Article 'Not really a 1 minute read, but a fun and interactive page from a16z' has no inline URL

articles.title: MUSK, GATES, AND ZUCKERBERG WALK INTO AN AI MEETING
articles.summary: Sounds like the start of a bad joke. Tech titans Elon Musk, Bill Gates, and Mark Zuckerberg met in Washington on Wednesday to talk about AI regulation. They were joined by founders from leading AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI. Hopefully, we hear some insight from the meeting soon!

articles.title: SOFTWARE TRANSCENDS BORDERS—WHY NOT MONEY?
articles.summary: Global money movement is a really complex topic. It’s really a lot like language. Each country grew up with its own system and way of communicating with money, and we’re still in the phase of translating that into a global language.

articles.title: THE WILD WEST OF E-COMMERCE
articles.summary: In light of Klaviyo’s S-1 filing, this article dives into the e-commerce space and the infrastructure that runs it. Much of the plumbing behind e-commerce is invisible to the consumer. Amazon’s FBA, Klaviyo’s marketing, Shopify storefronts, and wholesale marketplaces like Faire all enable the gears of e-commerce to turn without the consumer knowing about it.
A comprehensive read covering how to map your funnel, set up tooling, and start tracking the right data from marketing activities at your B2B startup.

articles.title: HELPFUL BOARDS
articles.summary: Board meetings can often be a huge waste of time. A board member should be your confidant. They should tell hard truths and play the long game. Here’s a helpful breakdown of the signals that indicate whether you’ve got a good board or not.

articles.title: GENERATIVE AI’S ACT TWO
articles.summary: Generative AI’s first act of developing foundational models and novelty apps is coming to an end. In its place comes Act Two: where generative AI solves human problems end-to-end.

articles.title: FAILING BETTER
articles.summary: Startups fail all the time, but not all of them fail gracefully. The core things to think about if you need to close shop include your team, your accounts payable, and your customers. Make sure you preserve the relationship with your team, don’t screw small businesses you owe money to, and don’t kick customers who may rely on your product or service to the curb.

articles.title: HOW TO AVOID DOING FRAUD
articles.summary: The most important rule of business is to never lie about your metrics. False metrics quickly spiral out of control, and you’ll begin to layer on fraud after fraud to keep the lie alive. Before you know it, the only thing that exists is the lie you’ve told and not the company behind it.

articles.title: THE CAR THAT WAS ALLERGIC TO VANILLA ICE CREAM
articles.summary: Customer feedback can sometimes bamboozle you. How on earth did they encounter that problem? Oftentimes it’s a case of getting to the root cause, which might have nothing to do with how the problem was first presented to you by your user.

articles.title: Not really a 1 minute read, but a fun and interactive page from a16z
articles.summary: that breaks down the global payment and fintech network, highlighting some of the key players across the globe.

articles.title: AUTOMATE (BUT AUTOMATE LAST)
articles.summary: Before you go automating things, make sure all unnecessary elements are removed first.
